title: minuartia viscosa subsp. confertiflora
intro: Minuartia viscosa subsp. confertiflora, also known as sticky catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is a perennial herb native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its sticky stems and clusters of small, white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing and the treatment of gastrointestinal issues. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
